message:
test(vtparse): expand from 27 to 69 unit tests for VT parser and CSI types (wa-34t0h)

Add 42 new tests covering the full public surface of the vtparse crate:

CsiParam type (8 tests):
- Default trait yields Integer(0)
- as_integer() returns Some for Integer variant, None for P variant
- Debug formatting for both Integer and P variants
- Display formatting for both variants
- Clone + PartialEq correctness across variants
- Hash consistency (equal values produce equal hashes)

VTParser state machine (3 tests):
- New parser starts in ground state (is_ground() == true)
- ESC byte transitions parser out of ground state
- Complete CSI sequence returns parser to ground state

parse_byte equivalence (1 test):
- Byte-at-a-time parsing produces identical VTAction sequence as
  bulk parse() for a representative SGR color + reset sequence

CSI dispatch sequences (8 tests):
- Cursor movement: CUU (up), CUD (down), CUF (forward)
- Cursor positioning: CUP (row;col H)
- Erase operations: ED (erase display), EL (erase line)
- SGR attributes: multiple params (1;31;42m), reset (0m)

C0 control characters (6 tests):
- LF (0x0a), CR (0x0d), HT (0x09), BS (0x08), BEL (0x07)
- Bare ESC (0x1b) produces no output (incomplete sequence)

ESC dispatch sequences (4 tests):
- RI (reverse index, ESC M)
- DECSC (save cursor, ESC 7), DECRC (restore cursor, ESC 8)
- Intermediate byte handling (ESC ( B for G0 charset designation)

OSC dispatch (2 tests):
- Multi-parameter OSC (10;rgb:ff/00/ff with BEL terminator)
- Single-parameter OSC

CollectingVTActor (2 tests):
- into_iter() and into_vec() produce correct collected actions

VTAction derive traits (3 tests):
- Clone produces equal values
- Debug output contains variant name
- Cross-variant inequality (Print != ExecuteC0orC1)

Mixed content (2 tests):
- Text interleaved with CR+LF control characters
- Multiple CSI sequences with text between (bold on, text, bold off)

Multi-byte UTF-8 (2 tests):
- 4-byte emoji (U+1F600 😀) parsed as single Print action
- CJK character (中) parsed as single Print action

Empty input (1 test):
- Zero-length input produces no actions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
